entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor genotypes used to study root exudation and bacterial utilization

entities.name: ROOT EXUDATION
entities.description: The release of soluble exudates from the roots of seedlings

entities.name: DIAZOTROPHIC BACTERIA
entities.description: Nitrogen-fixing bacteria that can utilize root exudates for growth

entities.name: SOLUBLE ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The organic carbon compounds released as part of root exudates

entities.name: ACETYLENE REDUCTION ACTIVITY
entities.description: A technique used to measure the nitrogen-fixing activity of plant-associated bacteria

entities.name: AZOSPIRILLUM LIPOFERUM 4ABL
entities.description: A specific strain of diazotrophic bacteria used in the study

entities.name: AXENIC GROWTH
entities.description: The growth of seedlings in a sterile environment to ensure no other organisms are present

relationships.description: Sorghum seedlings produce soluble root exudates
Diazotrophic bacteria utilize root exudates for growth
Root exudates consist of soluble organic carbon
Azospirillum lipoferum 4ABL is a specific strain of diazotrophic bacteria
The nitrogen-fixing activity of Azospirillum lipoferum 4ABL was measured using acetylene reduction activity
The ability of Azospirillum lipoferum 4ABL to utilize root exudates was evaluated
Sorghum seedlings were grown under axenic conditions
Axenic growth was used to measure the amount of soluble organic carbon exuded by seedlings
